title: Container running as root user
description: Dockerfile explicitly sets USER root or never changes from root. Containers running as root have unnecessary privileges that facilitate host escape and lateral movement.
remediation: Add a non-root USER instruction before ENTRYPOINT/CMD: RUN addgroup -S appgroup && adduser -S appuser -G appgroup && USER appuser

title: Secrets passed via build ARG instruction
description: Build arguments containing sensitive values are persisted in image layer metadata and can be extracted with `docker history --no-trunc`. Use BuildKit secret mounts instead.
remediation: Use BuildKit secret mounts: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret cat /run/secrets/mysecret. Build with: docker build --secret id=mysecret,src=secret.txt .

title: Base image uses :latest or no tag
description: Using :latest or omitting a tag makes builds non-reproducible. Upstream changes or supply-chain compromises will silently propagate. Pin to a specific version or SHA256 digest.
remediation: Pin base images to a specific version: FROM node:20.11.1-alpine3.19. For maximum immutability, pin by digest: FROM node@sha256:abc123...

title: Exposing privileged port in container
description: Exposing privileged ports (below 1024) typically requires root privileges. This often forces running the container as root, which is a security risk. Use ports above 1024 and remap if needed.
remediation: Use non-privileged ports (e.g., 8080 instead of 80, 8443 instead of 443) and remap at deployment: docker run -p 80:8080 myapp

title: ADD used instead of COPY for local files
description: ADD has implicit tar extraction and remote URL fetch capabilities that can introduce unexpected behavior or security risks. Use COPY for local files as it has transparent, predictable semantics.
remediation: Replace ADD with COPY for local files: COPY ./src /app/src. Only use ADD when tar auto-extraction is explicitly needed.
